goal: The two core logging flows read from and write to the database, with validation and proper states — no more in-memory seed data.
why: Until the owner service log and mechanic job log actually persist to Supabase, the product is a static prototype — making the two flows real is the demo's entire pitch.
success_criteria: An owner logs a service (type + date + place); it persists to service_entries (kind = owner_log) and survives a full refresh.
The owner's service history reads from the database, sorted newest-first, with localized service labels in EL and EN.
Every async owner/mechanic view has loading, empty, and error states in both languages.
A mechanic logs a job against a plate; it persists (kind = workshop_job) and appears in that vehicle's history, including the owner's view.
The mechanic 'saved' confirmation reflects the persisted row (no transient-only fake), reading recent entries from the DB.
Every mutation goes through a 'use server' action in src/lib/service-log.ts, verifies the user with getClaims() server-side, and is Zod-validated; reads are RLS-scoped (no service-role key).
No t.seedLog / t.mechanicSeed in-component arrays remain wired into the rendered logs.

tasks.title: Service-log data layer (Zod schemas + server actions + read queries)
acceptance_criteria: logOwnerService while signed out returns an auth error and writes nothing — verifies the user with getClaims() server-side before any insert.
A successful owner service inserts one service_entries row with kind = 'owner_log', logged_by = auth.uid(), a language-stable canonical code in description, place, and service_date.
A successful mechanic job inserts one service_entries row with kind = 'workshop_job', resolving the typed plate to vehicles.id and erroring with a human message on unknown plate.
localizeServiceCode returns the Greek label for lang='el' and the English label for lang='en' for the same stored code.
tasks.action: npm i zod. Create src/lib/services.ts (SERVICE_CODES = 10 canonical EN strings index-aligned with t.serviceOptions; localizeServiceCode). Create src/lib/service-log.ts (top-level use server; async createClient; zod). Each action verifies the user via getClaims(), safeParses FormData, then inserts: logOwnerService -> kind=owner_log (code+place+date); logMechanicJob resolves uppercased plate to vehicles.id (error if unknown) -> kind=workshop_job. Reads RLS-scoped, newest-first.

tasks.title: Owner ServiceLog reads/writes the DB (+ place input, states)
acceptance_criteria: An owner picks a service type, date, and where it was done, taps Log, and the entry appears immediately and persists across a full refresh.
The history reads from the DB sorted newest-first; switching EL/EN re-localizes each entry's service label via localizeServiceCode.
With no entries, the history shows the reassuring empty state in the active language, not a blank list.
A failed save shows a human inline error in the active language; the submit button shows a saving state while pending.
tasks.action: Make src/app/page.tsx an async Server Component calling getOwnerVehicleWithEntries() and passing {vehicle,entries} to ServiceLog. ServiceLog.tsx (use client): remove t.seedLog + useState persistence; render entries via localizeServiceCode; useActionState(logOwnerService) submitting canonical serviceCode + place + serviceDate; add a place input (existing class, t.placeLabel); add saving/empty/error states. Add new keys to el+en + the Translation type in i18n.ts.

tasks.title: Mechanic MechanicLog reads/writes the DB (+ persisted saved confirmation, states)
acceptance_criteria: A mechanic types a plate, picks a job type and date, taps Add to history; a kind='workshop_job' row persists and the recent list survives refresh.
The saved confirmation appears only after the row actually persisted, not as optimistic-only client state.
A job logged against the owner's plate (ΚΧΡ 412) appears in the owner's service history — one shared record.
An unknown plate shows a human, actionable error in the active language; the recent list shows the empty state when there are no jobs.
tasks.action: Make src/app/mechanic/page.tsx an async Server Component calling getMechanicRecentEntries() and passing entries to MechanicLog. MechanicLog.tsx (use client): remove t.mechanicSeed + added merge; render entries via localizeServiceCode; replace addEntry + justAdded/setTimeout with useActionState(logMechanicJob) submitting plate + serviceCode + serviceDate; ok->recordedTo, unknown_plate->error, pending/empty/load states. Add new keys to el+en + the Translation type.
